title: Joshua: Covenant Renewal
objective: Deepen biblical-theological understanding of joshua: covenant renewal and its place in redemptive history.

required_passages.text: Selected key passages for this unit segment, read in full during lesson delivery.

segments.audio_script: Today we study joshua: covenant renewal and trace God's covenant faithfulness.
Listen to the assigned passage and note covenant themes, divine actions, and human response.
We place this text in its historical setting, canonical location, and literary flow.
We analyze theological meaning, covenant development, and connections to the wider biblical story.
Key themes include God's holiness, covenant faithfulness, judgment and mercy, and redemptive purpose.

Review question: What choice did Joshua present to Israel in the covenant renewal at Shechem in Joshua 24? Answer: choose whom you will serve — either the gods your fathers served or the LORD — and Joshua declared that he and his house would serve the LORD.
segments.correct_answer: choose whom you will serve — the ancestral gods or the LORD
acceptable_variants: choose this day whom you will serve
serve the gods your fathers served or the LORD — joshua said he would serve the LORD
the choice between false gods and Yahweh at Shechem

segments.audio_script: Carry this lesson into prayer, worship, and careful Scripture reading as we continue the course.

vocabulary.definition: Joshua 24's covenant ceremony at Shechem where all Israel pledged loyalty to the LORD after the conquest; the people's repeated declarations foreshadow their later failure to keep the commitment
The site of Joshua's covenant assembly and Israel's covenant renewal; Abraham built his first altar there in Canaan (Genesis 12:6) and Jacob purchased land there
